document.title: PBRER for Cardiozan (Rivaximab Sodium) 10mg/20mg
document.description: Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report per ICH E2C(R2) for an oral Factor Xa inhibitor. Reporting period Apr 2023 - Mar 2024. Key safety focus: newly identified hepatotoxicity risk, ongoing thrombocytopenia signal, closed QT prolongation signal.

nodes.title: Title Page and Table of Contents
nodes.summary: Cover page with drug name, MAH, reporting period. Detailed TOC listing all sections and appendices with page references.

nodes.title: 1. Introduction
nodes.summary: Cardiozan is a Factor Xa inhibitor for NVAF stroke prevention, DVT/PE treatment and prevention. Doses 10-20mg. First approved EU Mar 2018. 6th PBRER. Pediatric PIP study CZ-PED-401 ongoing.

nodes.title: 2. Worldwide Marketing Authorization Status
nodes.summary: Approved in 78 countries. Pending in Brazil (ANVISA) and South Korea (MFDS). Japan PMDA query on DVT/PE indication. References Section 7 for signal impact.

nodes.title: 3. Actions Taken for Safety Reasons
nodes.summary: Regulatory: EMA SmPC update for hepatotoxicity warning (Jun 2023), FDA REMS modification (Aug 2023), Health Canada DHPC on thrombocytopenia (Oct 2023). MAH: RMP v8.0 update, post-marketing study CZ-HEP-501 initiated, educational materials to 45,000 HCPs. References Section 7.1.

nodes.title: 4. Changes to Reference Safety Information (CCDS)
nodes.summary: CCDS v12.0 updates: added severe hepatic impairment contraindication (Child-Pugh C), hepatotoxicity warning with ALT monitoring thresholds (discontinue if >5x ULN or >3x with symptoms), hepatocellular injury as uncommon ADR, thrombocytopenia reclassified to uncommon, CYP3A4/P-gp interaction warning (+160% exposure). References Appendix B Table B-3.

nodes.title: 5. Estimated Exposure and Use Patterns
nodes.summary: Cumulative: 2.4M patient-years. Current period: 520,000 PY. By indication: 60% NVAF, 18% DVT, 12% PE, 10% prevention. By region: 42% Europe, 30% NA, 20% APAC. Age: 65-74 largest (41%). Dose: 70% on 20mg. Special populations: 12% renal impairment, 3% hepatic, 15% concomitant antiplatelet. References Section 7.1 for hepatotoxicity exposure.

nodes.title: 6. Presentation of Data
nodes.summary: 14,820 ICSRs total (60% spontaneous). 3,705 serious (25%), rate 7.1/1000 PY. SAE tables: hepatobiliary +38% increase. 67 fatal cases: 23 ICH, 12 hepatic failure (up from 4), 11 GI hemorrhage. Literature: 342 publications including DILI studies (Chen, Yamamoto), thrombocytopenia mechanism (Rodriguez), efficacy (CARDIO-STROKE, CARDIO-VTE). References Appendix A for cases, Appendix B Table B-1.

sub_nodes.title: 6.1 Reference Information and ICSR Overview
sub_nodes.summary: CCDS v12.0 as reference. 14,820 ICSRs: 60% spontaneous, 20% clinical, 10% literature, 10% other. 3,705 serious (25%). Reporting rate 7.1/1000 PY (consistent with prior 6.5-7.8 range). Refers to Appendix B Table B-1.

sub_nodes.title: 6.2 Cumulative Summary of Serious Adverse Events
sub_nodes.summary: SAE tables by SOC. Hepatobiliary highest increase (+38%): 523 this period, DILI 198 new (387 cumulative). Blood disorders: thrombocytopenia 187 new (623 cumulative). Fatal: 67 total, hepatic failure 12 (up from 4). References Section 7.1 and Appendix A.

sub_nodes.title: 6.3 Cumulative Summary from Literature
sub_nodes.summary: 342 relevant publications. Key: Chen et al. DILI case series (15 cases, risk factors: age>65, statins, NAFLD, hepatocellular 73%), Yamamoto cohort (0.42% incidence, OR 2.3 vs warfarin), Rodriguez thrombocytopenia (anti-PF4 antibodies), CARDIO-STROKE (62% RRR, NNT=24), CARDIO-VTE (2.1% vs 8.7% recurrence).

nodes.title: 7. Signal Evaluation
nodes.summary: Three signals: (1) NEW hepatotoxicity/DILI - CONFIRMED, moved to identified risk, PRR 2.8, 198 new cases, 12 fatal, dechallenge+ 83.6%, rechallenge+ 72.7%; (2) ONGOING thrombocytopenia - immune mechanism suspected, anti-PF4 antibodies, study CZ-PLT-001; (3) CLOSED QT prolongation - refuted by TQT study (3.2ms < 10ms threshold), confounded by concomitant drugs.

sub_nodes.title: 7.1 New Signal: Hepatotoxicity (DILI)
sub_nodes.summary: CONFIRMED signal. PRR 2.8 (2.4-3.3) vs other DOACs. 198 new cases (387 cumulative): mean age 68.4, 58% female, median onset 42 days, 78% on 20mg. Outcomes: 67.7% recovered, 6.1% fatal. WHO-UMC: 12 certain, 89 probable, 78 possible. Dechallenge+ 83.6%, rechallenge+ 72.7%. References Appendix A for case narratives. Risk factors continued next page.

sub_nodes.title: 7.2 Ongoing Signal: Thrombocytopenia
sub_nodes.summary: 187 new cases (623 cumulative), rate 0.28->0.36/1000 PY. Severity: 45% Grade 1, 28% Grade 2, 18% Grade 3, 9% Grade 4 (3 fatal). Anti-PF4 antibodies in 8/12 tested = HIT-like mechanism. Risk factors: heparin OR 3.2, prior HIT OR 5.7, autoimmune OR 2.1. Study CZ-PLT-001 ongoing. References Section 8.2, Appendix B Table B-4.

sub_nodes.title: 7.3 Closed Signal: QT Prolongation
sub_nodes.summary: REFUTED. TQT study CZ-QT-201 (n=240): ddQTcF +3.2ms at 60mg supratherapeutic (below 10ms threshold). Phase 3 ECG (n=8,450): -0.8ms, no signal. 23 reports confounded: 18/23 concomitant QT drugs (amiodarone/sotalol), 4/23 long QT syndrome, 1/23 hypokalemia. Signal closed Dec 2023. No CCDS changes.

nodes.title: 8. Risk Characterization
nodes.summary: Identified risks: major bleeding, ICH, GI hemorrhage, hepatotoxicity/DILI (NEW), anemia, hypersensitivity. Hepatotoxicity detail: 0.38/1000 PY spontaneous, 0.42% true incidence, 6.1% case fatality, RR 2.3 vs warfarin. Risk factors: age>65, liver disease, statins, CYP3A4 inhibitors. Potential risks: thrombocytopenia, ILD, SJS, embryo-fetal. Missing: pediatric, pregnancy, severe renal, long-term, body weight.

sub_nodes.title: 8.1 Identified Risks
sub_nodes.summary: Six identified risks listed. Hepatotoxicity NEW (was potential): incidence 0.38/1000 PY (spontaneous) to 0.42% true (Yamamoto), case fatality 6.1%, RR 2.3 vs warfarin. Risk factors: age>65, liver disease (NAFLD/hepatitis), statins, CYP3A4 inhibitors. Risk minimization: CCDS update, DHPC, study CZ-HEP-501, educational materials.

sub_nodes.title: 8.2 Potential Risks and 8.3 Missing Information
sub_nodes.summary: Potential: thrombocytopenia immune (CZ-PLT-001), ILD (12 cases), SJS (4 cases), embryo-fetal. Missing info: pediatric (CZ-PED-401), pregnancy (CARDIO-PREG registry), severe renal (PK planned), long-term >5yr (CARDIO-REAL), body weight (CZ-BW-201). References Appendix B Tables B-5 to B-8.

nodes.title: 9. Benefit Evaluation
nodes.summary: NVAF: 21% RRR stroke/SE vs warfarin (CARDIO-AF), 62% RRR vs placebo (CARDIO-STROKE), 33% ICH reduction, NNT=24. DVT/PE: non-inferior efficacy, 46% major bleeding reduction, superior net clinical benefit. Real-world: CARDIO-REAL (n=28,450) confirms trials. DOAC comparison: ranked 2nd stroke, 1st ICH prevention. Convenience: once-daily, no INR.

nodes.title: 10. Benefit-Risk Analysis
nodes.summary: B-R FAVORABLE all indications. NVAF: stroke benefit 8.3/1000 PY vs hepatic fatality risk 0.023/1000 PY. DVT/PE: bleeding reduction key. Special populations: elderly net benefit maintained, renal 10mg adequate, hepatic Child-Pugh C contraindicated. Reassess after CZ-HEP-501 and CZ-PLT-001.

nodes.title: 11. Conclusions and Recommendations
nodes.summary: B-R favorable. Hepatotoxicity now identified risk (387 cases, PRR 2.8). Thrombocytopenia ongoing (anti-PF4). QT closed/refuted. No new signals. Recommendations: enhanced monitoring, RMP v8.0 submission, CZ-HEP-501 complete Q4-2024, CZ-PLT-001 interim Q3-2024, next PBRER Jun 2025. No indication/dose changes.

nodes.title: Appendix A: Line Listings of ICSRs
nodes.summary: Individual case narratives. Fatal hepatic failure (n=12) with RUCAM scores: CZ-2023-00891 (72F, RUCAM 9, Hy's Law, statin+amlodipine, died Day 52), CZ-2023-01204 (81M, RUCAM 7, NAFLD+ritonavir CYP3A4, died Day 28), CZ-2023-01567 (67F, RUCAM 6, hepatorenal, died Day 112). Also ICH fatals (n=23), GI hemorrhage fatals (n=11).

nodes.title: Appendix B: Cumulative Summary Tabulations
nodes.summary: Tables: B-1 all ICSRs by SOC (14,820), B-2 reporter type (66.6% HCP), B-3 PK interactions (ketoconazole +160%, ritonavir +153%, rifampicin -50%), B-4 thrombocytopenia by severity/risk factor, B-5 to B-8 risk minimization effectiveness.

nodes.title: Appendix C: Literature Search Strategy
nodes.summary: Searched PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, WHO Newsletter, Reactions Weekly. 1,247 hits -> 892 deduplicated -> 342 relevant -> 28 new safety info -> 15 requiring ICSR. Key pubs: Chen DILI, Yamamoto hepatotox, Rodriguez thrombocytopenia, Martinez CARDIO-STROKE, Wilson CARDIO-VTE, Thompson DOAC meta-analysis.
